{
  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
  "record_type": "coverage_assessment",
  "id": "senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21",
  "name": "Senolytics coverage repair assessment",

  "hallmark_id": "cellular_senescence",
  "assessment_type": "coverage_repair",
  "assessed_at": "2026-06-28T18:10:14Z",
  "coverage_verdict": "adequate",
  "coverage_confidence": "moderate",
  "summary": "The senolytics track has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and pilot human studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. D+Q bone safety extraction now includes registry aggregates, full-text publication-table preferred-term rows beyond headache, and registry/article discontinuation fields; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk records now cite retained PubMed source snapshots for abstract-level effect and safety details, with the legacy IPF tolerability result reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. Registry surveillance remains incomplete because ClinicalTrials.gov snapshots for deferred fisetin frailty registries could not be fetched in the worker, and the review landscape remains incomplete because no systematic-review or meta-analysis source was screened. Full article/table safety and efficacy extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplement material remains incomplete.",
  "evidence_categories": [
    {
      "category": "human_interventional",
      "coverage_level": "adequate",
      "rationale": "Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF pilot, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk now have at least abstract-located extraction for sample, dosing, timing, or effect details.",
      "source_ids": [
        "pmid-31542391",
        "pmid-36857968",
        "pmid-38956196",
        "pmid-40010154"
      ],
      "finding_ids": [

        "dq-bone-rct-primary-null-subgroup-biomarker-signal",
        "dq-ad-risk-pilot-cognition-mobility-signal"
      ]
    },
    {
      "category": "animal_lifespan_or_healthspan",

      "rationale": "Safety-oriented records now include the D+Q bone registry aggregate counts, full-text adverse-event table extraction, structured preferred-term rows for headache, diarrhea, nausea, constipation, gastrointestinal reflux, vertigo, fatigue, and body pain, and registry/article discontinuation fields. IPF and AD-risk safety slices now cite PubMed source snapshots for abstract-level safety statements, and DKD now has a source-snapshot-backed abstract limitation record. Cross-human safety remains thin because DKD, IPF, and AD-risk still need full article/table safety extraction and harmonized event definitions.",
      "source_ids": [

    },

  "summary": "The idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis randomized pilot abstract reports 12 participants randomized 1:1 to D+Q or placebo, complete scheduled dosing and assessment completion, more non-serious adverse events in the D+Q arm, no D+Q-related serious adverse events, and no meaningful between-arm differences in explored clinical measures.",
  "source_id": "pmid-36857968",
  "study_id": "dq-ipf-randomized-pilot",
  "intervention_ids": [
    "dasatinib-quercetin"
  ],
  "track_ids": [

  ],
  "hallmark_ids": [

    "chronic_inflammation"
  ],
  "endpoint_category": "safety",
  "direction": "mixed",
  "evidence_tier": "human_functional",
  "confidence": "low",
  "statement": "The randomized IPF pilot is useful for feasibility, tolerability, and endpoint-boundary evidence: all scheduled doses and planned assessments were completed, D+Q had more non-serious adverse events than placebo, and the trial was too small to support efficacy inference.",
  "population_or_model": "Adults with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis",
  "time_horizon": "3-week intervention period and planned pilot assessments",
  "caveats": [
    "Treat as safety and feasibility evidence unless a later synthesis verifies a powered efficacy endpoint.",
    "Functional outcomes should remain separate from aging-healthspan claims.",
    "Article-table extraction is still needed for term-level adverse events and clinical-measure details beyond the abstract."
  ],
  "tags": [
    "senolytics",
    "dasatinib-quercetin",
    "human",
    "safety",
    "idiopathic-pulmonary-fibrosis",
    "pilot"
  ],

    }
  ],
  "target_hallmark_ids": [

  ]

{
  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
  "record_type": "evidence_review",
  "id": "coverage-gap-senolytics-exact-effect-extraction-followup-2-repair-synthesis-boundary",
  "candidate_change_id": "coverage-gap-senolytics-exact-effect-extraction-followup-2-repair",
  "review_lane": "synthesis_boundary",
  "reviewer_kind": "supervisor_agent",
  "reviewer_id": "self-healing-candidate-review-coverage-gap-senolytics-exact-effect-extraction-followup-2-repair-synthesis-boundary",
  "status": "complete",
  "verdict": "accept",
  "blocking": false,
  "summary": "The reviewed candidate keeps synthesis boundaries intact: it limits the IPF update to abstract-located feasibility and tolerability evidence, treats DKD/IPF/AD-risk extraction as abstract-level pending full-text/table reconciliation, preserves D+Q bone supplement and cross-human safety debt, and does not convert pilot disease-specific signals into longevity efficacy claims.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "finding_id": "synthesis-boundary-pilot-efficacy-limits",
      "severity": "note",
      "category": "interpretation_overreach",
      "claim_or_issue": "The updated IPF finding, outcome, and result describe N=12 pilot feasibility and tolerability, explicitly noting that the trial is too small for efficacy inference and that disease-specific IPF outcomes do not establish general longevity benefit.",
      "why_it_matters": "The candidate avoids synthesizing a small disease-specific pilot into a broader senolytic efficacy or healthspan claim.",
      "recommended_action": "No revision required; preserve the existing pilot, safety, and endpoint-boundary caveats during any later synthesis.",
      "resolution_status": "closed"
    },
    {
      "finding_id": "synthesis-boundary-coverage-debt-visible",
      "severity": "note",
      "category": "missing_caveat",
      "claim_or_issue": "The coverage assessment labels the senolytics vertical slice as adequate while still listing full-text/table extraction debt for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, D+Q bone supplement material, registry surveillance breadth, and review-landscape screening.",
      "why_it_matters": "Keeping those limitations explicit prevents the coverage assessment from being used as a formal cross-human efficacy or safety synthesis.",
      "recommended_action": "No revision required; keep the documented debt and the block on cross-human safety synthesis until definitions, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness fields are harmonized.",
      "resolution_status": "closed"
    }
  ]
}
